firstCutover.requiredAcknowledgement: I acknowledge this first hosted cutover is forward-only; v0.17.4 must never run against the migrated pilot database; recovery is maintenance, forward-fix, or restore to a new cell until a compatible rollback anchor is established.

migrations.notes: Pinned v0.17.4 schema baseline; no rollback target is implied.
Installs or relocates unaccent; requires extension privileges but does not rewrite application rows.
Renames legacy users and adds Better Auth memberships; v0.17.4 cannot enforce the new account boundary.
Rebuilds archive-scoped primary and foreign keys under access-exclusive locks; legacy key assumptions are unsafe.
Adds guided-research state; v0.17.4 writers can omit or overwrite state the hosted release must preserve.
Adds integration, snapshot, backup, sync, and job state; v0.17.4 cannot preserve or safely delete referenced records.
Adds a review index only; no row shape or application contract changes.
Adds upload-intent persistence and a supporting uniqueness constraint; existing writers remain structurally valid.
Adds nullable rights fields and private media tables with provenance constraints; existing integration rows remain valid.
Adds media write-claim ownership records; existing application tables are unchanged.
Adds pg_trgm, a defaulted search projection, and an index; existing integration writers remain valid.
Adds persisted dataset mode defaulted to pilot; v0.17.4 ignores the mode and can seed synthetic data into a pilot cell.
Adds the durable production release-fence state machine and explicitly denies its control table to public API roles; existing application tables and writers remain structurally valid.

Adds fail-closed private-beta invitation, exact legal acceptance, email-verification, immutable audit, operator-replay, and durable auth-limit state; existing tables and writers remain structurally valid.
Adds privacy-safe worker heartbeat and immutable participant data-operation evidence tables with explicit API-role denial; existing application tables and writers remain structurally valid.

Backfills non-PII UUIDs across archives, people, facts, sources, and cases; volatile defaults rewrite/lock them. Adds immutable-ID triggers/indexes, confidence guards, digest-only tokens, quotas, and security evidence.

Adds a deployment-global minimal-PII beta application table with fixed fields, consent and delivery invariants, 90-day retention, HMAC identities, explicit API-role denial, and no changes to existing writers.
Adds bounded server-only public-demo capacity, digest-only guest sessions, archive generations, AI leases, and fixed-schema 30-day events without changing existing application tables or writers.
Adds a server-only singleton public-demo usage counter with explicit API-role denial; existing application tables and writers remain structurally valid.

Widens the public-demo session notice-version CHECK to accept both the 2026-07-16 and 2026-07-20 notices so existing rows stay valid while new sessions record the Plausible-naming notice; no other tables or writers change.
